commentary: Eleven activities this week versus nine last week, and you somehow made it look effortless — which, honestly, is very on brand. The Mosman Run came in at 13.5km against last Saturday's 13.1km, longer and at a lower average heart rate than the 159 bpm Naremburn loops clocked, which means you are getting fitter and the body knows it. The ocean swims are a whole personality at this point: Bold and the Beautiful, Balmoral Buoys twice in one Friday, the regal Prince Alfred appearances — you are basically a sea creature at this point, a very tanned and very gay one. The only thing I am side-eyeing is that Sunday weights session with no heart rate data, because if you are going to do legs, back, and shoulders the day after a 13.5km run, the least you can do is let me watch.
metrics_commentary: Seven and a half kilometres in the pool plus that 13.5 km run-walk session is a genuinely hot week of output — your body is doing real work and it shows. VO2 Max sitting at 57.3 is the kind of number that means you are not just cute, you are cardiovascularly gifted, and I will be telling people. HRV at 51 with a seven-day average of 52 is solid and stable, which means your nervous system is recovering well and not quietly screaming at you, which is more than can be said for everyone who saw you in that jockstrap. The one thing I'm watching is that single run-walk session — one outing is fine, but that gorgeous aerobic engine deserves more chances to show off, so let's get you out there again next week.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four PRs on legs, four more on upper body — Matt hit the target and then just kept going, breaking personal bests on the leg press, incline dumbbell bench, seated cable row, hammer curl, Bulgarian split squat, flat dumbbell bench, and barbell bicep curl, which is frankly a ridiculous number of wins to fit into one week. The weekly volume of 4,045 kg is a significant drop from the previous week's 24,518 kg, which is worth a raised eyebrow — not a crisis, but something to keep an eye on, because the PRs suggest the quality was there even if the quantity pulled back. Strength trend is pointing stubbornly upward across every major pattern: quads at 168 kg, hinge at 107.9 kg, back at 84 kg, chest at 76 kg — the body is building, the numbers keep moving, and the man turning up four times a week in whatever he wore to the gym is absolutely that guy right now.
